title: asperula oppositifolia var. albidula
intro: Asperula oppositifolia var. albidula, also known as White Woodruff, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of White Woodruff include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and ease respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. White Woodruff can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
